findings.description: Design specifies synchronous payment processing (Stripe API call blocking) with async email notifications; trade-off documented (eventual consistency accepted for email delivery)
findings.suggested_action: Dev phase implements with proper error handling for Stripe timeouts; monitoring configured to track payment latency

architecture_diagram.data_flow_sequence: provided (user → API → Stripe → DB → feature flag → email queue)

payment_processing.rationale: Requirement specifies <2s latency; synchronous provides guaranteed consistency and immediate user feedback
payment_processing.tradeoff: User experiences Stripe API latency (~500ms); acceptable within budget; mitigated by retry + timeout handling

source_of_truth.rationale: Fast reads (<10ms); independent of Stripe; requirement targets <100ms API response time
source_of_truth.tradeoff: Eventual consistency (~5-60min lag); acceptable for subscription state changes (rare events)
source_of_truth.mitigation: Webhooks for real-time updates; daily cron job for reconciliation (Phase 4+)

feature_flag_caching.rationale: Fast flag lookups (<1ms); reduce DB load by 90%
feature_flag_caching.tradeoff: Max 5min delay in flag state change; acceptable for non-critical feature gating

authentication.implementation: CakePHP auth middleware validates token on every request

authorization.rule: User can only view/modify own subscriptions (user_id check)

rate_limiting.rule: 100 req/minute per user; 1000 req/minute per IP

api_response_time.strategy: Async email job (no blocking I/O except Stripe)

subscription_creation_e2e.strategy: Stripe SLA <500ms; index on (user_id) for fast lookup

db_query_latency.strategy: Index on (user_id, status); select specific columns; eager loading for joins

stripe_api.error_handling: Stripe decline → 402; timeout → retry with backoff

uniqueness_constraints.payment_methods.stripe_payment_method_id: Map Stripe tokens to local records

deployment_strategy.rollback_plan: Disable feature flag → (optional) DB rollback if data corruption

security_tests.checklist: No card data logged, CSRF token validation, auth check on endpoints

tradeoffs_documented.tradeoff: User experiences Stripe latency (~500ms) vs. guaranteed consistency
tradeoffs_documented.rationale: Consistency critical; Stripe latency acceptable within <2s budget

tradeoffs_documented.tradeoff: Eventual consistency (~5-60min lag) vs. guaranteed real-time state
tradeoffs_documented.rationale: Fast reads (<10ms) critical; eventual consistency acceptable for subscription state

tradeoffs_documented.tradeoff: Max 5min lag in flag state vs. guaranteed current state
tradeoffs_documented.rationale: Performance benefit (90% fewer DB queries); lag acceptable for non-critical feature

compliance_checklist.authentication: ✓ JWT bearer tokens required on all endpoints
compliance_checklist.error_handling: ✓ Sensitive data never in error messages
compliance_checklist.logging: ✓ No API keys or payment tokens logged

architectural_risks_identified.impact: Payment processing blocked; users cannot subscribe
architectural_risks_identified.mitigation: Retry logic with exponential backoff; timeout after 30s; user-facing error message

architectural_risks_identified.risk: Webhook delivery failure (subscription never created despite charge)
architectural_risks_identified.impact: Subscription state mismatch; user sees no subscription despite payment
architectural_risks_identified.mitigation: Webhook signature verification + idempotency; reconciliation cron job (Phase 4+)

architectural_risks_identified.risk: Race condition (duplicate subscription if concurrent requests)
architectural_risks_identified.likelihood: Very low (prevented by DB unique constraint on user_id + active status)
architectural_risks_identified.mitigation: Unique constraint in schema; request deduplication (idempotency key in Phase 4+)

api_contract.endpoints_mapped: All 5 AC scenarios covered by API endpoints

architecture.components: 6 (API layer, Service layer, DB, Stripe, Email, Feature flags)

security.compliance_checklist: 8/8 items addressed (PCI, GDPR, CSRF, auth, rate-limit, etc.)
